ACS.source_name: U.S. Census ACS 5-year via Census Reporter API

variables.poverty_pct: ACS B17001 population below poverty / poverty universe
variables.uninsured_pct: ACS B27010 no health insurance coverage / coverage universe
variables.age_65_plus_pct: ACS B01001 population age 65+ / total population
variables.disability_pct: ACS B18101 population with a disability / civilian noninstitutionalized population
variables.transportation_access_proxy: ACS B08201 households with no vehicle available / households
variables.internet_access_gap_pct: ACS B28002 households with no internet access / households

ACS.notes: Census Reporter exposes ACS estimates without requiring a Census API key. For production, teams may swap in direct api.census.gov calls with an API key.

HRSA HPSA.source_name: HRSA Health Professional Shortage Area downloads

variables.hpsa_primary_care_score: Maximum HRSA primary care HPSA score by county
variables.hpsa_mental_health_score: Maximum HRSA mental health HPSA score by county
variables.hpsa_dental_score: Maximum HRSA dental HPSA score by county
variables.hpsa_*_designated: 1 when the county has at least one designated HPSA record

CMS Hospital Quality.source_name: CMS Provider Data: Hospital General Information

variables.hospital_count: CMS hospital facility count by county
variables.acute_care_hospitals: CMS acute care hospital count by county
variables.avg_hospital_star_rating: Average numeric CMS overall hospital rating by county

CMS Hospital Quality.notes: The CMS General Information file does not include hospital beds or a county-level readmission rate, so those exact fields remain documented fallback inputs.

notes: Default mode attempts real ACS and CDC PLACES data first. HRSA/CMS are integrated when available. A documented sample file fills fields not provided by those feeds.
